title: Measurement, SI Units, Dimensional Analysis, and Uncertainty · Assumption Clinic
summary: Measure a room, select materials, convert specifications, and report tolerances for a community repair. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a unit-safe materials estimate and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose appropriate SI base and derived units and write their symbols correctly.
Convert quantities with unit factors that equal one and cancel dimensions explicitly.
Square or cube conversion factors when converting area and volume.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Unit Validation evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Unit Validation assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Use dimensional consistency to detect formula and data-entry errors before calculation. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Compare plausible approaches against the decision, constraints, evidence quality, and cost of being wrong.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: math beginner measurement units. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Unit Validation, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a unit-safe materials estimate.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Units are decorative labels added after the arithmetic. This fails the Unit Validation contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Recalculate the repaired case
steps.instruction: A rectangular working area measures 5 m by 3 m. Calculate its area.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Multiply perpendicular lengths and square the length unit: 5 m × 3 m.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Multiply perpendicular lengths and square the length unit: 5 m × 3 m.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes m² or m^2 or square meters.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Unit Validation applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use dimensional consistency to detect formula and data-entry errors before calculation.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
